description_long: This is a well-used hammer featuring a straight, cylindrical wooden handle with a weathered, brown finish. The handle shows signs of age and use, with visible discoloration and surface wear. The head of the hammer is made of dark metal, exhibiting a rectangular striking face and a wedge-shaped back, typical of a standard carpenter's hammer. The metal head has a matte, rough surface with visible rust and wear, indicating frequent use. No visible branding or logos are present on either the handle or the head. The overall appearance suggests a utilitarian tool commonly found in urban garages, workshops, or construction sites.
description: A weathered hammer with a straight wooden handle and a dark, worn metal head, showing signs of heavy use and rust.
description_view_0: The hammer is seen from the top, highlighting the rectangular metal head and the full length of the wooden handle.
description_view_1: A side profile reveals the straight wooden handle and the wedge-shaped metal head, with visible rust and wear.
description_view_2: The hammer is viewed from the bottom, showing the end of the handle and the broad striking face of the head.
description_view_3: A full side view displays the entire length of the handle and the flat striking face of the metal head.
attribute_handle_type: straight wooden handle with a cylindrical shape, visibly weathered and lacking ergonomic or grip enhancements
attribute_head_type: rectangular steel head with a flat striking face and a wedge-shaped back, typical of a standard carpenter's hammer
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: visible rust, discoloration, and surface wear on both the handle and the head
